projects.title: Arkaiv — AI-Powered Personal Operating System
projects.subtitle: React, Gemini, Sheets, OpenClaw
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.toolbox: React 19, Vite, Tailwind CSS 4, Express.js, Google Gemini AI, Google Sheets API, Google Calendar API, OpenClaw AI Agents (Scrum)
projects.techniques: Full-stack development, AI multi-model routing, Local-first data architecture, PKM, AI-orchestrated Scrum, PWA, Framer Motion, Recharts
projects.industries: Personal Productivity, Personal Finance, Self-Quantification, Creator Tools
projects.results: A complete personal OS with 8 integrated views backed by Gemini AI — local-first, PWA-capable, actively maintained at notes-dump-zeta.vercel.app.
projects.content:
## Arkaiv — Ngarsip Harian, Unpacked

> "How can I build one system that truly understands my daily life and helps me manage it better?"

In 2026, I created **Arkaiv** (short for *Ngarsip Harian* or Daily Archiving). It started as a simple place to store notes. Over time, it grew into my complete personal operating system.

Arkaiv connects 8 different views in one place: Money, Notes, Tasks, Shopping, Calendar, Focus, Library, and Summary. It uses Google Gemini AI and works with a local-first design synced to Google Sheets.

**Core Insights**

- One single app removes the stress of using many separate tools
- AI is most helpful when it quietly watches and gives useful suggestions at the right time
- Google Sheets can work very well as a simple, free, and easy-to-read storage
- Using the right AI model for each task keeps the app fast and low cost
- Managing AI agents with Scrum helped me build strong software quickly, even working alone
- Learning my own habits and creating focused work modes improved my daily consistency

### Insight #1: From Simple Notes to Full Life System

I used to switch between many apps like Notion, Excel, and habit trackers. It felt messy and tiring. Arkaiv began as a notes tool but slowly became one central place for everything in my daily life.

I chose a local-first approach. This means the app works fast in the browser and can be used offline. All data is also saved to Google Sheets as a backup. The data is easy to read and free to store.

### Insight #2: Making AI Simple and Smart

I built a smart system that chooses the best AI model for each job:

- Simple tasks use **Gemini Flash** (very fast and cheap)
- Complex tasks use **Gemini Pro** (more powerful)

The AI also learns my patterns. For example, if I always mark "Kopi Kenangan" as a coffee expense, it remembers and stops asking next time. This saves time and makes the app feel smoother.

### Insight #3: Focus and Daily Life Integration

One feature I really like is the Focus view. It turns my tasks into realistic time blocks and automatically adds them to my Google Calendar. This helps me protect time for deep work.

All views work together. The app can show how my spending, tasks, and schedule connect. It gives me clear warnings and suggestions based on my real situation.

### Insight #4: Building with AI Agents using Scrum

I acted as the project leader and worked with AI agents inside OpenClaw. We followed Scrum methods with clear plans, regular check-ins, and testing. This helped me create clean, well-documented code much faster than working alone.

### Tech & Design Highlights

- **Frontend:** React 19 + Vite 6 + Tailwind CSS with smooth animations
- **AI Engine:** Google Gemini with smart model selection
- **Data Storage:** Local-first design + Google Sheets sync
- **Integrations:** Google Calendar and Google login
- **Extra:** Works offline as a Progressive Web App (PWA)
- **Design:** Clean, calm, and easy to use every day

**Live Project:** [Arkaiv](https://notes-dump-zeta.vercel.app)

### Strategic Takeaways

Arkaiv shows that a personal tool built with care and modern AI can be better than many paid apps. It helped me develop skills in full-stack development, AI integration, and creating useful daily systems.

This project reflects how I like to work: intentional, connected, and always improving. It is now my main daily tool and is still actively maintained.

projects.title: Olist Ecommerce Satisfaction Visualization
projects.subtitle: Power BI, Visualization
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.content:
## Olist Case Study, Unpacked

> "What truly separates a satisfied customer from a deeply disappointed one?"

**Core Insight**
1. A healthy average can hide operational illness
2. Products and sellers aren't the real culprits
3. Delivery delays trigger dissatisfaction
4. The shipping phase is the true bottleneck
5. Delay hotspots are route-specific
6. High cost ≠ fast delivery

### Insight #1: Surface-Level Health Can Hide Internal Dysfunction
![Olist Dashboard](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68727945c3f561671347630c_1.png)

4.09 out of 5 looked fine. But averages lie — they mask extremes.

### Insight #2: It's Not the Products or Sellers
![Product Analysis](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872794533838b79611b3f0d_2.png)

Performance lines were flat — categories and sellers showed no red flags.

### Insight #3-6: Delivery Is the Real Problem
![On-Time vs Late](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68727945750d80a157082fbf_3.png) ![Logistics](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872794581428b13e9127d9a_4.png) ![Delay Matrix](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687279445fe95b0ea228de3e_5.png) ![Cost vs Time](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687279457d0489bf504f8247_7.png)

Shipping time ballooned from 8 to 27+ days. Only certain routes affected. Olist was overpaying for subpar service.

## Strategic Takeaways

In modern e-commerce, delivery isn't just a service — it's part of the product.

projects.title: Essential Oils Customer Satisfaction Analysis
projects.subtitle: Python, Machine Learning
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.techniques: Sentiment analysis, topic modeling, explainable AI, regression, Kano classification
projects.industries: Consumer wellness, essential oils, digital marketplaces
projects.results: Targeted product insights, data-driven quality improvements, novel review-to-roadmap pipeline.
projects.content:
## Case Study: Decoding Customer Satisfaction for Essential Oils

> "Can we extract strategic product insights purely from online reviews?"

23,000+ reviews from Tokopedia. Goal: identify what truly drives satisfaction.

**Core Insight**
- Aroma and packaging are key 5-star drivers
- Mid-tier buyers are highly price-sensitive
- Diffuser support is a "must" for premium consumers
- Kano Model + SHAP reveals segment-specific strategies

### Insight #1: What People Really Talk About
![BERTopic](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872210461b8e8a2b3c69f32_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20154556.png)
Scraped 23,068 reviews. BERTopic extracted 11 Customer Satisfaction Dimensions.

### Insight #2-3: What Drives Ratings
![Sentiment](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68721ee0b304a9ed752578c3_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20153724.png)
![MAE](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/68722109e8c17297c0014e28_MAE%20Model.png)
Aroma strongest positive SHAP impact. XGBoost: MAE 0.125.

### Segment Insights
![Low](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872226bb6ac4d56581cbd77_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20155216.png)
![Mid](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872226be740983f6e01f40d_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20155228.png)
![Premium](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/6872226b8df2ae8cbeaf1928_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20155238.png)

Feedback should be a compass for innovation, not noisy data.

projects.title: Telco Customer Churn Prediction
projects.subtitle: Python, EDA, Machine Learning
projects.author: Aruci Team (Bilal, Akhdan, Dendy)
projects.results: Lower churn, smarter targeting, service strategies rooted in behavioral data.
projects.content:
## Customer Churn, Unpacked

> "How can a telecom company turn churn into insight?"

7,000+ records from Jakarta and Bandung.

**Core Insight**
- New customers churn most in first 5 months
- Value-added service users stay longer
- High-end device owners are a churn risk
- Credit/pulsa users churn more
- Call center interaction reduces churn

### Insights with Charts
![Churn Early](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227ebb6ac4d56581e585d_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161413.png)
![Usage](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227eb93dde2a217c84db9_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161445.png)
![Device](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227eb630a6b555231f3d8_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161504.png)
![Payment](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227ebf48c8590a9a9ee7a_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161534.png)
![Call Center](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687227eb290c7dad4c1b09a1_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161552.png)
![Model](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/687117f971cd518b166221a9/687228302a3f1bf28668a00d_Screenshot%202025-07-12%20161729.png)

XGBoost balanced performance.

projects.title: Health Commodities Supply Chain by USAID Visualization
projects.subtitle: Tableau, Visualization
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.techniques: Operational dashboarding, multi-year KPI trend analysis
projects.results: Disrupted assumptions, data-backed narratives, strategic lessons.
projects.content:
## USAID Supply Chain Analysis, Unpacked

> "What's really happening inside the health commodities supply chain run by USAID?"

My first-ever visualization project. Dataset from Kaggle — simulated USAID supply chain.

**Core Insight**
- 2015 delivery volume collapse
- Air charter ate the budget
- RDC share shrinking
- Nigeria dominated distribution
- Reliability and cost efficiency feed each other

### Key Findings
The 2015 delivery cliff, air charter costs, RDC decline, and Nigeria's dominance painted a picture needing strategic attention.

### The Reliability-Cost Gap
I realized I was tracking volumes and costs but not answering what matters: reliability (consistency, on-time rate) and cost efficiency (weight, freight cost). This gap became the dashboard redesign foundation.

## Strategic Takeaways
A first draft dashboard is never the final answer. Ask: what question did I not answer? Then go back and answer it.

projects.title: Maintenance Schedule and History Visualization
projects.subtitle: Looker, Visualization
projects.author: Akhdan Hanif
projects.toolbox: Looker Studio, Script App, Google Spreadsheets
projects.techniques: Operational dashboarding, multi-year KPI trend analysis
projects.results: Visualization of maintenance schedule and report
projects.content:
## First Hands-on Looker Studio

Practice project with dummy maintenance data based on real machine failure history.

### Page #1: Maintenance Schedule Quick Overview
Color-coded grids for maintenance status. Google App Scripts button enables direct spreadsheet manipulation.

### Page #2: Maintenance Detailed Report
Bar charts: downtime per machine, failure type breakdown, workload by PIC. Tables and location charts for tracking issues.

projects.title: Mental Health Prediction App
projects.subtitle: Python, Machine Learning
projects.author: Yukai Mojikai Team (Izzan, Akhdan, Bagus, Rafi, Rifqi)
projects.results: Journaling App with Mental Health Analysis
projects.content:
## Mental State Classification Using BERT

Pipeline classifying user text into five mental sentiment categories using BERT and TensorFlow. Deployed on Google Cloud Run.

## Data Preprocessing
- Removed punctuation, numbers, stop words
- BERT tokenizer, padded to 53 tokens
- Attention masks for valid tokens

## Building the Model
Pre-trained BERT from HuggingFace + custom TensorFlow layers. Adam optimizer. Score: 88.4.

## Deployment
Google Cloud Run auto-scaling. REST endpoint: raw text in → sentiment classes out.

## Final Thoughts
End-to-end NLP pipeline. Built not just a model but a complete sentiment service for real-world use.
